text.title: Server-Side Tracking (CAPI and Offline Upload)
text.summary: Meta Conversions API Node.js implementation, deduplication with browser events, and Google offline conversion flow.

rule.statement: Always implement server-side tracking alongside browser pixels — never rely on browser pixels alone.
rule.rationale: Ad blockers, iOS App Tracking Transparency, and browser privacy features block 20-40% of browser pixel events. Server-side events pass directly from your server, bypassing all browser restrictions, and can include richer user data for better match rates.

example.note: event_id must match the browser pixel's eventID parameter to enable Meta's deduplication — same event_id from browser + server = counted once.

rule.statement: Set matching event_id values in both the browser fbq() call and the CAPI server event — mismatched IDs cause double-counting, inflating reported conversions by up to 2x.
rule.rationale: Meta deduplicates events within a 48-hour window when event_id matches; without this, the algorithm sees twice the conversions and bids accordingly, wasting budget.
